tex:
Here is part of the menu for Jamie's café.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|r|}
\hline
Menu & Price (\$) \\
\hline
Tea & 2.35 \\
Coffee & 3.40 \\
Lemonade & 1.80 \\
Cake & 4.45 \\
Biscuit & 0.85 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Sue has one tea and one cake.
\newline
Calculate how much she pays.
\newline

\$ 

(b) Derrick has one coffee and two biscuits.
\newline
How much change does he receive from a \$10 note?

\$ 

(c) Harriet works at the café for 34 hours each week.
\newline
She is paid \$8.25 for each hour.
\newline

(i) (d) Work out the amount she is paid each week.
\newline

\$ 

(e) One week she works 8 hours extra.
\newline
The extra hours are paid at 1.5 times her usual rate of \$8.25 for each hour.
\newline
Work out the total amount she is paid for that week.
\newline

\$ 

(f) Peter works these hours each week at the café.
\newline
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Day & Time \\
\hline
Monday & 08 30 to 16 00 \\
Tuesday & 10 00 to 17 00 \\
Thursday & 08 30 to 16 30 \\
Saturday & 08 00 to 18 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Work out the number of hours he works in one week.
\newline

 hours

(g) Jamie buys a clock for the café from Japan for 9395 yen.
\newline
The exchange rate is \$1 = 110.27 yen.
\newline
Work out the cost of the clock in dollars, correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\$ 

(h) Jamie invests \$12 000 at a rate of 5\% per year compound interest.
\newline
Calculate the value of his investment at the end of 3 years.
\newline

\$

_rawBody:
Here is part of the menu for Jamie's café.

\begin{tabular}{|l|r|}
\hline
Menu & Price (\$) \\
\hline
Tea & 2.35 \\
Coffee & 3.40 \\
Lemonade & 1.80 \\
Cake & 4.45 \\
Biscuit & 0.85 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{parts}
\item Sue has one tea and one cake.
Calculate how much she pays.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item Derrick has one coffee and two biscuits.
How much change does he receive from a \$10 note?

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item Harriet works at the café for 34 hours each week.
She is paid \$8.25 for each hour.

\begin{subparts}
\item Work out the amount she is paid each week.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item One week she works 8 hours extra.
The extra hours are paid at 1.5 times her usual rate of \$8.25 for each hour.
Work out the total amount she is paid for that week.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}
\end{subparts}

\item Peter works these hours each week at the café.
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Day & Time \\
\hline
Monday & 08 30 to 16 00 \\
Tuesday & 10 00 to 17 00 \\
Thursday & 08 30 to 16 30 \\
Saturday & 08 00 to 18 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Work out the number of hours he works in one week.

\AnswerLine[] hours
\Marks{2}

\item Jamie buys a clock for the café from Japan for 9395 yen.
The exchange rate is \$1 = 110.27 yen.
Work out the cost of the clock in dollars, correct to the nearest cent.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item Jamie invests \$12 000 at a rate of 5\% per year compound interest.
Calculate the value of his investment at the end of 3 years.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

qtype: order of operations, ordering FDP, number properties, roots
tex:
(a) Work out $48 \div 3 - 5 \times 2$.
\newline

(b) Insert one pair of brackets to make this statement correct.
\newline

$3 + 2 \times 12 - 4 = 19$

(c) Write the following in order, starting with the smallest.
\newline
$\dfrac{3}{4}$, $0.749$, $76\%$, $\dfrac{11}{15}$

 ,  ,  , 

(d) Find the value of
(i) (e) $\sqrt{265.69}$,

(f) $\sqrt{83}$.
\newline

(g) Write down the smallest prime number.
\newline

(h) Write down all the factors of 18.
\newline

 ,  ,  ,  ,  , 

(i) Write down a common factor of 16 and 72 that is greater than 2.
\newline

(j) Write $\dfrac{28}{140}$ as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

 / 

(k) Jeff and his friends win a prize.
\newline
Jeff's share is \$160 which is $\dfrac{5}{11}$ of the prize.
\newline
Work out the value of the prize.
\newline

\$

(i) the mean.

tex:
Fourteen students each take two tests in French, a speaking test and a written test.
\newline
The table shows the scores.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Speaking test & 10 & 13 & 48 & 30 & 35 & 18 & 41 & 40 & 22 & 28 & 20 & 44 & 37 & 46 \\
\hline
Written test & 24 & 44 & 51 & 39 & 45 & 29 & 56 & 20 & 39 & 49 & 33 & 52 & 44 & 52 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the scatter diagram.
\newline
The first ten points have been plotted for you.
\newline

(b) What type of correlation is shown in this scatter diagram?

(c) One student has a high score in the speaking test and a low score in the written test.
\newline
On the scatter diagram, put a ring around this point.
\newline

(d) On the scatter diagram, draw a line of best fit.
\newline

(e) Use your line of best fit to estimate a score in the written test for a student who scored 25 in the speaking test.
\newline

content.content: What type of correlation is shown in this scatter diagram?

One student has a high score in the speaking test and a low score in the written test.
\newline
On the scatter diagram, put a ring around this point.
\newline

On the scatter diagram, draw a line of best fit.
\newline

Use your line of best fit to estimate a score in the written test for a student who scored 25 in the speaking test.
\newline
